holdings.name: IRFC (Indian Railway Finance Corp)
holdings.description: The financial backbone of Indian Railways — IRFC borrows from markets at AAA-rated sovereign-backed rates and on-lends 100% to Ministry of Railways. Every rupee of rail capex flows through IRFC's balance sheet. Zero credit risk (Government of India guarantees repayment). ₹4.6 lakh crore+ loan book growing 15%+ annually. The purest proxy for rising rail capex in India — higher capex = higher IRFC revenue with near-zero default risk.

holdings.name: RVNL (Rail Vikas Nigam)
holdings.description: India's premier rail infrastructure construction PSU — executes doubling of track, electrification, bridges, tunnels, and new lines on behalf of Ministry of Railways. ₹85,000 Cr+ active order book. Revenue growing 25%+ YoY. Metro rail projects in addition to Indian Railways work. RVNL is transitioning from pure EPC contractor to developer — taking equity stakes in rail projects and earning annuity income over 30+ year concessions.

holdings.name: Container Corporation (CONCOR)
holdings.description: India's largest container logistics company — 60%+ domestic rail container market share. Operates 60+ inland container depots (ICDs) and container freight stations (CFS) nationwide. Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFC) are transformational: double-stack trains cut logistics costs by 25% and CONCOR volumes on DFC are 3x legacy route speeds. Privatisation stake sale (government owns 54%) remains a periodic narrative catalyst for re-rating.

holdings.name: Titagarh Rail Systems
holdings.description: India's largest private wagon manufacturer with 45%+ market share. 20,000+ wagons per year production capacity. Electric Multiple Unit (EMU) coaches and metro rail cars in Kolkata and Delhi. Titagarh Wagons (merged with Titagarh Firema) gives it pan-India + European passenger rail capability. ₹15,000 Cr+ order book from Indian Railways, Metro Rail Corp, and private freight operators.

holdings.name: IRCON International
holdings.description: Government EPC company specialising in railways, highways and urban infrastructure. ₹25,000 Cr+ order book in Indian rail projects plus international orders from Sri Lanka, Malaysia, Bangladesh and Nepal. IRCON's international rail projects are unique: building rail lines in neighbouring countries advances India's diplomatic infrastructure goals while generating substantial recurring revenue.

holdings.name: RailTel Corporation
holdings.description: India's only pan-India rail-aligned telecom infrastructure company — owns and operates 60,000 km of OFC (optical fibre cable) along rail tracks. RailWire broadband (last-mile connectivity), station Wi-Fi (6,000+ stations), data centres (RailTel Tier-III DC in Gurugram, Hyderabad) and enterprise connectivity services. Unique asset: OFC alongside every railway track in India — creating an unmatched backbone for digital India.

holdings.name: Jupiter Wagons
holdings.description: Fastest-growing wagon manufacturer — grew from 3,000 to 12,000+ wagons capacity in 3 years. Container wagons for DFC, BOXNHL high-speed goods wagons, passenger coach interiors. Light Rail Vehicle (LRV) business targeting metro and RRTS projects. Jupiter's aggressive capacity expansion signals management's confidence in the 10-year rail capex cycle. Lowest cost per wagon due to vertical integration.

holdings.name: Kernex Microsystems
holdings.description: India's only listed signalling and train collision avoidance technology company. KAVACH (the automatic train protection system mandated after Balasore tragedy for 34,000 km of rail) is Kernex-developed. Ministry of Railways has ₹18,000 Cr KAVACH deployment contract — Kernex is a direct beneficiary. Export potential to South Asia and Africa where Indian rail signalling standards are being adopted.

holdings.name: NBCC (India)
holdings.description: PSU construction company handling station redevelopment — 50 major stations under NMBS (National Monetization of Station) scheme. Gandhinagar, Ayodhya and Rani Kamlapati (Habibganj, Bhopal) stations are showpieces. NBCC earns project management consultancy (PMC) fees for project execution without balance-sheet risk. ₹10,000 Cr+ station redevelopment pipeline in next 5 years.

holdings.name: Texmaco Rail & Engineering
holdings.description: 100-year-old rail engineering company — wagons, coaches, bridges and hydromechanical structures. Texmaco Rail's track record across multiple rail capex cycles gives it institutional credibility with Railway Board for bulk orders. Hydraulic doors and vestibules for Vande Bharat trains. Diversified into defence (armoured vehicles) and hydro infrastructure for revenue stability.
